free_text: 14N(alpha,d)16O at 30 MeV
MP tandem accelerator at the Williams
Laboratory was used in the experiment
Adenine target with thickness of 150 mu-g/cm2 on a 10
mu-g/cm2 carbon backing.
In some measurements a 14N gas target was used with
pressure of 300 torr.
Outgoing particles were detected
in three detector telescopes, each consisting of a
surface-barrier detector with thickness of >30 mu-m
and a 3mm Si(Li) detector.
Particles from the adenine target were also
detected in a double-focussing Enge split-pole magnetic
spectrometer. Signals from position-sensitive detectors
in the focal plane were analyzed by the on-line
computer.
Source of uncertainties not described
Compiled by S.H.
